And the reason we call this advertised occupancy is instead of just true occupancy.
Um Again, that has to do with what we can see on the property website.
So this typically happens in a lease up, but properties can have different kind of advertising strategies on their website.
Maybe a property has 31 bedrooms available they don't want to make or they don't want to look desperate.
So they'll only put 10 of them available on the property website.
Our system can only account for the 10 that are made available on the property website.

And in terms of that, I I always say that what we're reflecting here, what we're showing an apartment IQ is exactly what a renter would see whenever he or she visits your property website or visits the competitors.

Um, in terms of transactional data, leasing information, since we're tracking each unit individually, if that unit leaves the property website, we assume that it has been leased.

Unit 123 is not, we assume that that unit has been leased.
